channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: West Indies players return home on commercial flights after ICC charter delays
item.description: West Indies cricket team players have started returning from India on commercial flights after their chartered plane was cancelled due to international airspace closures amid ongoing tensions in the M...

item.title: The impact of Glasgow’s historic building fires on community identity and belonging
item.description: The recent fire at Glasgow’s Union Corner building, adjacent to Central Station, has stirred strong emotions among Glaswegians, recalling previous tragedies like the fires at the Glasgow School of Art...

item.title: Johannesburg investigates 16 cases of fraudulent municipal land transfers
item.description: The Johannesburg Property Company (JPC) has launched 16 fraud cases with the police following the discovery that several city-owned properties were transferred to private individuals and companies wit...

item.title: Rappler Daily Wrap: Oil Surge Due to Gulf Conflict, Meralco Rate Hike, Ressa Acquittal Recommendation, Herbosa Graft Complaint, and Russian Cyber Campaign
item.description: On March 10, 2026, Rappler's daily news wrap highlighted several major developments. Globally, oil prices surged to their highest levels since 2022 amid escalating conflict in the Middle East involvin...

item.title: Wife Of Missing NELAN Engineer Accuses Cover-Up Linked To Former Ebonyi Governor Umahi In 2021 Disappearance Case
item.description: This matter dey about the mysterious disappearance of five engineers wey dey work for Nelan Consultants since November 2021. Dem travel go Abakaliki, Ebonyi State, to supervise the Abakaliki Ring Road...

item.title: First Impressions of Apple’s Affordable MacBook Neo
item.description: Trevor Long provides a first look at Apple's newly launched MacBook Neo, a more affordable addition to the MacBook lineup. The review highlights the laptop's design, performance, and overall usability...

item.title: Inmate charged with murder of Soham killer Ian Huntley following prison attack
item.description: A 43-year-old prisoner named Anthony Russell, currently incarcerated at HMP Frankland in County Durham, has been charged with the murder of Ian Huntley. Huntley, aged 52 and serving life imprisonment ...

item.title: Calls for Australia to ensure the safety of Iranian women's football team during Asian Cup stay
item.description: The Iranian women's football team faced international attention and safety concerns after refusing to sing their national anthem ahead of their match against South Korea in the Women's Asian Cup. This...

item.title: High Court directs immediate return of R82 million to uThukela Municipality amid legal dispute
item.description: The Pietermaritzburg High Court has issued an urgent order for the immediate return of R82 million to the uThukela District Municipality, which had been withdrawn from its accounts by service provider...

item.title: Toronto police investigate firearm discharge at U.S. Consulate amid recent local attacks
item.description: Toronto police are investigating an incident in which shots were fired at the U.S. Consulate in downtown Toronto early Tuesday morning, near University Avenue and Queen Street West. Authorities confir...

item.title: India Reviews Naval Escorts for Commercial Ships in Strait of Hormuz Due to Middle East Tensions
item.description: India is evaluating the deployment of its naval vessels to escort Indian commercial ships through the Strait of Hormuz amid the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. This strategic waterway has become ...

item.title: Brig. Gen. Hanzel Marantan appointed acting Calabarzon police chief
item.description: Brig. Gen. Hanzel Marantan has officially assumed his role as acting chief of the Calabarzon police, a day after his appointment. He succeeds Brig. Gen. Paul Kenneth Lucas, who had led the region's po...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Congratulates Mojtaba Khamenei, Emphasises Peace and Bilateral Cooperation with Iran
item.description: ISLAMABAD: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Tuesday extended his congratulations to Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei on assuming the position of Supreme Leader of the Islamic Republic of Iran. In his messag...

item.title: Rescue teams search for diver missing after being swept by Cheploch Gorge river
item.description: Rescue teams in Elgeyo Marakwet County are working hard to find a diver who was swept away by a swollen river at Cheploch Gorge. The incident happened during a search operation when heavy rains made t...

item.title: Tanzania Women Judges Reaffirm Commitment to Combat Gender-Based Violence During International Day Commemoration
item.description: In Dodoma, women judges from Tanzania joined others around the world to mark the International Day of Women Judges 2026. The event was organised by the Tanzania Women Judges Association (TAWJA) under ...

item.title: PM directs investigation into delays in Kalambo Hospital construction
item.description: In Sumbawanga, Prime Minister Mwigulu Nchemba has instructed the Prevention and Combating of Corruption Bureau (PCCB) in Rukwa to investigate why the construction of Kalambo District Hospital is still...

item.title: Apple at 50: Key Innovations and Notable Failures
item.description: Celebrating fifty years since its founding in 1976, Apple has profoundly shaped the technology landscape with products that have transformed computing, music, and communication. Its early successes be...

item.title: Remembering Mosiuoa Lekota: Freedom Fighter and Devoted Family Man
item.description: Mosiuoa “Terror” Lekota, co-founder of COPE, has passed away at the age of 77. Born as the first son of Mapiloko Meshack Lekota and Mamosiuoa Aphaphia Lekota, he grew up valuing family, culture, and e...

item.title: Baguio and Other Remote Areas Experience Increased Fuel Costs Due to Logistics
item.description: Diesel prices in Baguio City have climbed to around P90 per liter, significantly higher than in other parts of the country, largely because of the city's distance from the nearest oil depot in Poro Po...

item.title: Transporters raise concerns over new port tariffs and impact on trade costs in Tanzania
item.description: Transporters and traders in Tanzania are expressing serious concerns about the new domestic cargo charges introduced by the Tanzania Ports Authority (TPA), which started on March 8, 2025. They warn th...
